items.title: Update: The Boston Celtics and Jordan Walsh have agreed to a 3-year, $16 million extension, which includes two years fully guaranteed and a third-year team option for the 2029-30 season, sources said. Boston had picked up Walsh’s $2.4 million team option for the upcoming season.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: Update: The Boston Celtics and Jordan Walsh have agreed to a 3-year, $16 million extension, which includes two years fully guaranteed and a third-year team option for the 2029-30 season, sources said. Boston had picked up Walsh’s $2.4 million team option for the upcoming season.

items.body_excerpt: Boston Celtics forward Jordan Walsh has agreed to a three-year, $15 million contract extension with the franchise through the 2029-30 season, sources tell ESPN. Boston picked up Walsh's $2.4M option on next season and adds three more years for their 2023 second-rounder.

items.title: Asked Lynne Roberts if she feels there’s added pressure on the Sparks to figure it out post-ASB: “I don’t think there was ever not pressure to figure it out. It doesn’t change. … In terms of pressure, this is what we do. ... I want to succeed, and we will.”
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Asked Lynne Roberts if she feels there’s added pressure on the Sparks to figure it out post-ASB: “I don’t think there was ever not pressure to figure it out. It doesn’t change. … In terms of pressure, this is what we do. ... I want to succeed, and we will.”

items.title: Asked Dearica Hamby how the Sparks can use this All-Star break to get refreshed: “We showed last year we were able to really respond after the All-Star break and having a complete, healthy team. Looking forward to getting [Kelsey Plum] back and the second half of basketball.”
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: Asked Dearica Hamby how the Sparks can use this All-Star break to get refreshed: “We showed last year we were able to really respond after the All-Star break and having a complete, healthy team. Looking forward to getting [Kelsey Plum] back and the second half of basketball.”

items.title: Asked Lynne Roberts last night why the live ball turnovers have been such a problem for the Sparks lately: “If I knew, I’d fix it. I don’t know. … Those are the ones that just, you know, they had 20 points off our turnovers. That’s the game.”

items.title: Not every open-source project wants more contributors right now, and that's fine. Four shapes maintainers run — walled castle, clique, university, hippie commune — and your `CONTRIBUTING.md` is saying the quiet part out loud. No wrong answer, just an honest one.

items.title: The Sky are 9-18, but the season has been defined as much by how they've lost as by how often.
items.author: Drew
items.body_excerpt:
The Sky are 9-18, but the season has been defined as much by how they've lost as by how often.

Thirteen of their 18 losses have come by 10 points or fewer. 

Despite sitting 13th in the WNBA standings, they own the league's eighth-best point differential (-2.2).

items.title: Hollander POTD Gary Payton. Mr Mean was Oregon St PG & college POY in 89-90. Drafted 2nd in 90 by SEA. Took a few years but turned into all-star in 93-94 & stayed one thru 03. Peaked at 24.2 ppg in 99-00 and was 95-96 DPOY. Later, played for MIL, BOS, LAL & MIA.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst

items.title: SGA's dad on why SGA loves OKC: "There ain't nothing to do in OKC. All Shai wants to do is hoop, it's a perfect fit. As opposed to like being somewhere where you know there's nightlife... or in Toronto where everybody's gonna be on your ass: 'Hey, can I get tickets? Can I get this, can I get that?'"
items.author: New-Process-6463
items.body_excerpt: People ask like, 'What is Shai doing in OKC and how does he like it?' He loves it. Because all Shai wants to do is hoop. There ain't nothing to do in OKC. All Shai wants to do is hoop, it's a perfect fit. Yeah, you get what I'm trying to say? As opposed to like being somewhere…
